steps.title: Find ST using the area formula
steps.content:
Since angle T is a right angle, sides RT and ST are perpendicular legs. Substituting into the area formula:

$$792 = \frac{1}{2} \times 72 \times ST$$

$$ST = 22$$

steps.title: Identify the similar triangle relationship
steps.content:
Since $LK \parallel RT$ with L on $RS$ and K on $ST$, triangle $SKL$ is similar to triangle $SRT$. The scale factor is:

$$\frac{LK}{RT} = \frac{24}{72} = \frac{1}{3}$$

In similar triangles, all corresponding linear measurements share the same scale factor.

steps.title: Calculate KT using the similarity ratio
steps.content:
For sides extending from vertex S, the similarity ratio applies:

$$\frac{SK}{ST} = \frac{1}{3}$$

$$SK = \frac{22}{3}$$

Since K lies on $ST$:

$$KT = ST - SK = 22 - \frac{22}{3} = \frac{44}{3} \approx 14.67$$
